title: UTF-8 Missing and from readme
author: jabcreations
body:
So git is lame as it wants me to clone the entire project just to make a commit for you to read, review and accept.

I sent the following via the subject and body:

“Hello World”

It malformed after being received for both. The answer is simple, add the following line after declaring `$mail`:

`$mail->CharSet = 'UTF-8';`

I'd recommend adding that line to any/all instances where `$mail` is declared such as the readme file.

title: DSNConfigurator maps smtps:// to STARTTLS instead of implicit TLS

title: .user.ini sendmail_from override broken in 7.0.2
author: gmariani
body:
There is an error with this patch. I have hundreds of sites hosted on Nexcess (now LiquidWeb) and Nexcess by defaults hosts them on unique random domains (i.e. [random].nxcli.io), but aliases the live domain to your site. Because of this, the server configure sendmail path looks like this:

`/sbin/sendmail -t -i -f postmaster@[random].nxcli.io`

In order to have mail sent from the website validate, we have a .user.ini file set sendmail_from on every site with something like this:

`sendmail_from=wordpress@example.com`

This method worked up until WordPress 7.0 which includes PHPMailer 7.0.2 and pull request #3283 . The one line causing this issue is this:

`if (self::isShellSafe($this->Sender) && strpos($phpmailer_path, ' -f') === false) {`

Because of the way it creates the command it creates a command with two -f like this:

`/sbin/sendmail -t -i -f postmaster@[random].nxcli.io -oi -fwordpress@example.com -t`

Which is then ignored by the new guard. But we need both in order to override the original address. Because of this change, none of our sites can send mail anymore and it's causing huge problems. My solution for now is to override the logic with this patch:

```
add_action('phpmailer_init', function ($phpmailer) {
    $phpmailer->isSendmail();
    $sendmail_from_value = ini_get('sendmail_from');
    if (empty($sendmail_from_value)) return;
    $phpmailer->Sender = $sendmail_from_value;
}, 999);
```

isSendmail() reads in the .ini settings but crucially switches it from 'mail' to 'sendmail' which then uses sendmailSend() which doesn't have the guard in place. I am then able to re-set the From address from the .user.ini and send mail successfully again. Maybe a better patch would be to replace the existing -f flag instead of concatenating it? Something like this maybe?

```
if (self::isShellSafe($this->Sender)) {
    $phpmailer_path = preg_replace('/\s-f\S+/', '', $phpmailer_path);
    $params = sprintf('-f%s', $this->Sender);
}
```

That way it's the best of both worlds. For confirmation I did some testing as well, the _gf suffix is Gravity Forms, the _ini suffix is from .user.ini:

In v6.9.4
```
From: wordpress_gf@example.com
Received-SPF: Pass (mailfrom) identity=mailfrom; client-ip=###.###.###.###; helo=cloudhost-#######.us-midwest-1.nxcli.net; envelope-from=wordpress_ini@example.com; receiver=<UNKNOWN>
```

In v7.0
```
From: wordpress_gf@example.com
Received-SPF: None (mailfrom) identity=mailfrom; client-ip=###.###.###.###; helo=cloudhost--#######..us-midwest-1.nxcli.net; envelope-from=postmaster@[random].nxcli.io; receiver=<UNKNOWN>
```

In v7.0 when I revert the one line to what it was in v6.9.4
```
From: wordpress_gf@example.com
Received-SPF: Pass (mailfrom) identity=mailfrom; client-ip=###.###.###.###; helo=cloudhost--#######.us-midwest-1.nxcli.net; envelope-from=wordpress_ini@example.com; receiver=<UNKNOWN>
```

So I can say definitively that one line is causing the problem. Hopefully this can be patched and then integrated upstream into WordPress 7.0.1 or something. Thanks for your attention to this!

title: PHP IMAP Installed: Using simplified address parser is not recommended. Install the PHP IMAP extension for full RFC822 parsing.
author: jabcreations
body:
Hi,

So after updating I see this new check is in place with a PHP warning:

Using simplified address parser is not recommended. Install the PHP IMAP extension for full RFC822 parsing.
PHPMailer.php
1356

Okay, well enabling IMAP in php.ini is one of my setup tasks when setting up a new server:

php.ini:
extension=imap

PHP info:
IMAP c-Client Version 2007f

So it looks like it _should_ work though I'm not sure what else to check for? Latest stable version of PHPMailer 7.1.1. I've confirmed that emails are getting sent and received.

title: SMTP Error: Could not authenticate

title: Strict checks on Encoding values should be case-insensitive
author: imclean557
body:
Please check these things before submitting your issue:

- [X] *Read the error message* you're seeing - it often tells you what is wrong, and may contain useful links & instructions
- [X] Make sure you're using the latest version of PHPMailer
- [X] Check that your problem is not dealt with in [the troubleshooting guide](https://github.com/PHPMailer/PHPMailer/wiki/Troubleshooting), especially if you're having problems connecting to Gmail or GoDaddy
- [X] Include sufficient code to reproduce your problem
- [ ] If you're having an SMTP issue, include the debug output generated with `SMTPDebug = 2` set
- [ ] If you have a question about how to use PHPMailer (rather than reporting a bug in it), tag a question on Stack Overflow with `phpmailer`, but [**search first**](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/phpmailer)!

# Problem description

PHPMailer validates Encoding values against a pre-defined list with all values being lower case.  This then results in an error if the Encoding set isn't all lowercase.  Example error:  "Unknown encoding:8Bit"

Encoding is case-insensitive:  https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2045.html#section-6.1

# Code to reproduce

Set `$this->encoding to "8Bit";` on a PHPMailer object.

Reported in this Drupal issue:  https://www.drupal.org/project/phpmailer_smtp/issues/3590468

# Debug output

author: schroef

title: Security valuable trouble
author: animer3009
body:
I have an SMTP-enabled Microsoft email account and a simple contact page (secured by Google reCAPTCHA).

By design, when someone passes the CAPTCHA and presses the Send button, the script sends an email to my own address.

The problem is that for the second time, someone has somehow obtained my SMTP credentials and is sending spam using my account. Unfortunately, I don’t have the option to restrict SMTP access via a firewall.

I’ve done my best to validate and sanitize all input, but it didn’t help. The second time it took several months, and they still managed to do it again.

When I check the sent emails, I can literally see that they are sending emails with my SMTP credentials written openly in the subject line.

In my code, the recipient is hardcoded as the same SMTP email address. There is no user input field for it. So I have no idea how they are able to send emails unless they somehow obtained my credentials.

Has anyone experienced something similar or have any idea what might be happening?

I’ve spent a lot of time reviewing security. I even set up a new server and reviewed the simple page’s code, but I still have no idea what’s going on.

title: Unable to authenticate Microsoft 365 using Oauth2

body:

I tried to follow all of the guidance on github

I am getting an error message:

Invalid response received from Authorization Server. Expected JSON.(uiserver)

I would really appreciate some help as to how to troubleshoot this as I find the error message incomprehensible.
Of course, if you need more information from me to be able to help, please let me know. I wasn't sure what else to say.

title: Get_Oauth_Token.php fails with Error 500
author: galexmay
body:
I had previously done a composer require phpmailer/phpmailer which seemed successful.
Initially, I did not change get_oauth_token.php. I downloaded it and told Azure its url as the redirect URI.
I opened get_oauth_token.php in a web browser. I filled in the form and submitted it. I was asked to log in to my account. I then got "This page isn’t working bladelaw.co.uk is currently unable to handle this request. HTTP ERROR 500". I had the option to reload and when I hit that button, I got "Provider missing".
I then tried to follow the instructions if you don't want to use the web form. I filled in credentials in the script and ran it using php from the command line. I got the webform html returned. I commented out that section and I then got nothing at all.

Any help as to what might be causing this problem would be very gratefully received.

title: Request For Guidance: PHPMailer V.6.10 communicating with localhost postfix only
author: MHCreations
body:
We Have unfortunately had to move servers from Exim (which I loved) to Postfix / Plesk . 

The new server has Postfix, PHP and PHPMailer V6.10.0 set up. 

Postfix is set on port 25 and emails are working correcting, PHPMailer will communicate with Postfix on Post 25 and will always use Authentication to send emails from a server based email address. All email generating comms between Postfix and PHPMailer are Localhost only. 

Example set up:

```
$mail = new PHPMailer();
$mail->SMTPDebug = 4;
$mail->isHTML(false);
$mail->Timeout = 20;
$mail->isSMTP();
$mail->SMTPAutoTLS = true;
$mail->Username = "sales@my-domain.org";
$mail->Password = 'my-secure-password';
$mail->SMTPAuth = true;
$mail->AuthType = 'LOGIN'; // This works. 
$mail->Port = 25; // Only 25 seems to work; other ports need to have authorisation set up by Postfix
$mail->WordWrap = 80;
$mail->From = "sales@my-domain.org";
$mail->FromName = "TESTER";
$mail->Subject = $subject;
$mail->Body = $body;
```

The above works fine for emails on port 25 to the local server to send out; but changing the port to any other value -even that Postfix is set up on (eg 2626) results in failure to authenticate. Postfix master.cf was set up with a new row for the new port; 2626. So postfix should be running on both ports . 

```
$mail->Port = 2626;
```

The thing is, firewalls should not be blocking this as the approach is local - PHPMailer on the same server is trying to access Postfix on the same server using the Port 2626 but result is: 

> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 Connection: opening to localhost:2626, timeout=20, options=array()
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 Connection: opened
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SMTP INBOUND: "220 server-domain.com ESMTP Postfix"
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SERVER -> CLIENT: 220 server-domain.com ESMTP Postfix
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 CLIENT -> SERVER: EHLO www.my-domain.org
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SMTP INBOUND: "250-mhccorefour.co.uk"
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SMTP INBOUND: "250-PIPELINING"
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SMTP INBOUND: "250-SIZE 25067520"
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SMTP INBOUND: "250-ETRN"
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SMTP INBOUND: "250-STARTTLS"
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SMTP INBOUND: "250-XFORWARD NAME ADDR PROTO HELO SOURCE PORT IDENT"
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SMTP INBOUND: "250-ENHANCEDSTATUSCODES"
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SMTP INBOUND: "250-8BITMIME"
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SMTP INBOUND: "250-DSN"
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SMTP INBOUND: "250 CHUNKING"
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SERVER -> CLIENT: 250-server-domain.com250-PIPELINING250-SIZE 25067520250-ETRN250-STARTTLS250-XFORWARD NAME ADDR PROTO HELO SOURCE PORT IDENT250-ENHANCEDSTATUSCODES250-8BITMIME250-DSN250 CHUNKING
> SMTP Error: Could not authenticate.
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 CLIENT -> SERVER: QUIT
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SMTP INBOUND: "221 2.0.0 Bye"
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 SERVER -> CLIENT: 221 2.0.0 Bye
> 2026-01-27 16:35:16 Connection: closed
> SMTP connect() failed. https://github.com/PHPMailer/PHPMailer/wiki/Troubleshooting
> done

The issue is "Could Not Authenticate" on Port 2626 but on Port 25 it works fine. Postfix should be working on both ports.  I am unsure if this is a PHPMailer issue (probably not, to be honest) or I need to set something else up on Postfix side of things?

Any Help Appreciated. I have read the Troubleshooting documentation but can't see an approach to solving.
